Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a class or course within the school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

name.description: The name of the class (e.g., '10th Grade Math').

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in a class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

number.description: The student's unique number within the class.

password.description: Hashed password of the student. Should be stored securely.

needsPasswordChange.description: Indicates whether the student needs to change their password on the next login.

risks.description: References to RiskFactors. (Relationship: Student N:N RiskFactor)

projectPreferences.description: An array of lesson IDs representing the student's project preferences.

assignedLesson.description: Reference to Lesson. (Relationship: Student 1:1 Lesson, optional)

referrals.description: References to other Student entities representing referrals. (Relationship: Student 1:N Student (Self-Referential))

fcmTokens.description: An array of Firebase Cloud Messaging tokens for sending push notifications.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a lesson or project that students can be assigned to.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

name.description: The name of the lesson (e.g., 'Math Project').

quota.description: The maximum number of students that can be assigned to this lesson.

RiskFactor.title: RiskFactor
RiskFactor.description: Represents a risk factor that can affect a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RiskFactor entity.

label.description: The label or name of the risk factor (e.g., 'Broken Family').

weight.description: A numerical weight representing the severity of the risk factor.

InfoForm.title: InfoForm
InfoForm.description: Represents a detailed information form for a student.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message exchanged between users (teachers and students).

senderId.description: Reference to User (Teacher or Student). The ID of the message sender.

receiverId.description: Reference to User (Teacher or Student). The ID of the message recipient.

timestamp.description: A timestamp indicating when the message was sent (e.g., Unix timestamp).

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeacherProfile entity.

schoolName.description: The name of the school where the teacher works.

principalName.description: The name of the school principal (for reports).

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question in the question bank.

definition.description: Stores class information.  Only teachers can create/modify.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class.

definition.description: Stores student data. Authorization is based on class membership.

params.description: The unique identifier for the student.

definition.description: Stores lessons associated with a class.  Teachers manage lessons.

params.name: classId

params.name: lessonId

definition.description: Stores messages between students and teachers.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher.

definition.description: Indicates teacher role using document existence.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher.

definition.description: Stores learning objectives defined by teachers.
Stores questions for the question bank.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure prioritizes authorization independence and simplifies security rules by denormalizing authorization data. It uses path-based ownership and membership maps where appropriate.

*   **/classes:** Stores class information. Publicly readable, but only teachers can create/modify.
*   **/classes/{classId}/students:** Stores student data.  Authorization independence is achieved by denormalizing class membership for students. Teachers are granted write access based on their role.
*   **/classes/{classId}/lessons:** Stores lessons and their quota. Teachers are granted write access based on their role.
*   **/riskFactors:** Stores risk factors. Teachers can add/edit/delete.
*   **/students/{studentId}/infoForms:** Stores info forms. Students and teachers can read/write student's own data.
*   **/students/{studentId}/messages:** Stores messages between students and teachers. Secure read/write based on sender/receiver IDs. To support secure listing, messages are stored in subcollections owned by each student.
*   **/teachers/{teacherId}:** Stores teacher profile data. Only the teacher can modify their own profile. Enables storing the teacher profile information (name, branch, schoolName, principalName) needed for Word document generation.
*   **/roles_teacher/{teacherId}:**  Indicates teacher role using document existence. Simplifies teacher role checks in security rules without needing document content.

**Authorization Independence:**  The structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization data.  For example, student documents under `/classes/{classId}/students` do not need to fetch the class document to determine if a teacher has write access.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   Secure `list` operations are achieved by:
    *   Segregating data with different access needs into separate collections or subcollections.
    *   Using path-based ownership for private data (e.g., `/students/{studentId}/infoForms`).
    *   Leveraging the membership map pattern where appropriate.

**Invariants:**

*   Ownership is enforced through path-based ownership (e.g., `/students/{studentId}/infoForms`).
*   Timestamps are supported in messages.  Consider using server timestamps for increased reliability.
*   Denormalized data is managed through atomic operations (transactions/batches) to maintain consistency.
